mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that boosts corporate revenue projections across multiple sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales fails to yield a positive rank IC over 60-day horizons, or returns are negative in more than 50% of co-occurring periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and falling factory utilization that trigger short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not predict negative 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal generates a negative rank IC when volatility indices exceed 25.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 2.5% force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the mechanism fails to predict direction when core inflation stays below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation shocks that undermine earnings stability, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence fails to predict negative returns, or rank IC turns positive when the unemployment rate change is driven by demographic factors rather than job losses.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts signal strong consumer confidence and upstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative rank IC during periods of restrictive mortgage rates.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of elevated inflation signals stagflationary pressures that erode real corporate profits and trigger rapid equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns when inflation exceeds 2.5%, or the signal generates positive rank IC during supply-constrained regimes.
